name: Seafood Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Shredded reduced-fat Monterey Jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein shrimp, set aside.
2. Bring 4 cups water to a boil in a large saucepan; add shrimp and cook for 3-5 minutes or until shrimp turn pink. Drain well and set aside.
3. Melt margarine in a small saucepan over low heat; add flour, stirring until smooth.
4. Gradually add chicken broth and 1/2 cup water to the saucepan, stirring constantly. Cook over medium heat until the mixture is slightly thickened and bubbly. Remove from heat and let stand for 5 minutes.
5. Gently stir in sour cream, jalapeno pepper, and salt.
6. Pour about 3/4 cup of sauce into the bottom of a 13x9x2 inch baking dish coated with cooking spray.
7. Place the remaining sauce aside. In a container for an electric blender, process cottage cheese until smooth, stopping occasionally to scrape down the sides.
8. Transfer cottage cheese mixture to a medium bowl and stir in shrimp, crabmeat, chiles, and Monterey Jack cheese.
9. Spoon about 1/3 cup of seafood mixture across the center of each tortilla.
10. Roll up the tortillas, seam side down, over the sauce in the dish.
11. Pour the remaining sauce over the tortillas.
12. Bake at 350 degrees for 25 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
13. Spoon salsa cruda across the center of the tortillas and garnish with fresh cilantro sprigs if desired.
Salsa Cruda:
1 cup peeled, seeded, and chopped tomatoes
1/2 cup chopped onion
1/4 cup no-salt-added tomato juice
2 tablespoons chopped fresh cilantro
1/8 teaspoon salt
1 small jalapeno pepper, seeded and minced. Combine all ingredients in a small bowl, stirring well. Yield: 1 1/2 cups.
